title: Review consumer idempotency and dead-lettering
description: Read a consumer and report whether redelivery is safe, offsets commit after effects, and poison events are dead-lettered.
body: Use when validating an event consumer. Actions: inspect the handler for dedup on the event id committed atomically with the effect; verify the offset/ack is committed only after the effect is durable; check that retries are bounded and a dead-letter topic exists; confirm ordering assumptions match the partition key. Do not assume the broker dedups. Report the consumer location and classify gaps as non-idempotent, commit-before-effect, no-DLT, or wrong-ordering-assumption. This skill is read-only against non-production. Validation signals include a redelivery-single-effect path and a DLT path. Risk boundary: a non-idempotent consumer on a sensitive event is a blocking finding to escalate.
